title: Was Gendo the one that caused Second Impact
display_title: Was Gendo the one that caused Second Impact
wikitext:
[]: Was Gendo the one that caused Second Impact?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Tue Feb 04, 2014 7:28 pm Hello all, it's been a while. This has been nagging at me for quite a while, and while it'd make horrendous narrative sense, I'm not certain that there's much evidence one way or another. The reason I ask is because of three things: 1) During one episode they specifically mention Gendo having left Antarctica the day before 2I, and there's literally no other reason to point this particular detail out. EDIT: This would also make him the only other survivor of the Katsuragi Expedition aside from Misato, which raises at least a few questions. 2) Gendo was already working for SEELE, iirc, and would be in a perfect position to, I don't know, screw things up so the lance went too far and awakened Adam. 3) Considering what Gendo's ultimate goal was (especially in the manga, which makes this plainer) it would make sense if he was the one who started the chain of events that turned Adam into a controllable embryo. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 04, 2014 7:52 pm He can very much be said to be "responsible" in that he probably arranged what would happen and that the expedition was there for the exact purpose of being manipulated into blowing themselves up, with Gendo pulling the strngs in the shadows, but there's frankly no need for him to sabotage anything personally. If even keel frequented there personally, the place should have been crawling with lesser seele operatives who could be sacrifced to pull the trigger, but sabotage isn't necessary for things to go wrong - just don't tell the scientist what exactly they're handling and what will happen if they poke the wrong thing, and subtly guide their experimental arrangements to what you want. Besides, the only reason Gendo lived is that he got the fuck away *before* they started the final experiment. The very fact that he's not vaporized proved he was involved, but that much should be obvious to first-time viewers... there's no need for him to have taken direct action for this. Also, he wouldn't have been planning on using the embryo before Yui's death. He, Yui and Fuyu were still out to prevent/subvert it at the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 04, 2014 7:53 pm Gendo wasn't working for Seele until Yui was sucked into the Evangelion. While it's an interesting theory, I think it was the fault of those working on board the expedition or Seele themselves causing things to go awry.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 04, 2014 8:16 pm Gendo was working for Seele when he got the job down in Antarctica, and probably well before that. Hell, it's entirely possible he was already a person they were interested in before he met Yui. I mean the rumors Fuyu heard were that's why they hooked up, but that would imply Gendo already knew a good deal about them which seems unlikely unless he was already a person they were interested in.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 04, 2014 8:38 pm Gendo's always been a master at sticking his nose where it doesn't belong. I wouldn't be surprised if he had done some snooping on Seele without them expressly wishing to recruit him. Besides, why would he be working for Seele? IIRC, he didn't even sign on for the HIC until 2004.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Tue Feb 04, 2014 11:27 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Gendo's always been a master at sticking his nose where it doesn't belong. I wouldn't be surprised if he had done some snooping on Seele without them expressly wishing to recruit him. Besides, why would he be working for Seele? IIRC, he didn't even sign on for the HIC until 2004.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Tue Feb 04, 2014 11:35 pm there's something about the lance being sent back to the dead sea and returned just in time for the contact experiment Maybe Seele sent a replica instead of the real one and the lance was supposed to keep Adam from starting any impacts, and since the Katsuragui Expedition Scientists had a fake one without knowing everything went wrong, Gendo either knew about this or suspected that Seele did something fishy with the lance and bugged out.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Feb 05, 2014 4:46 am ^ Since the same lance was retrieved during the series as was confirmed as the original one, it's clearly the real deal which was sent to the Katsuragi Expedition. Now for Gendo himself, according to the Classified Information : Not long after he became a member of Seele, he went to the South Pole to be an auditor for the Katsuragi Investigation Team.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 05, 2014 11:36 am All the events in question are neatly packaged in episode 21, so that's well worth watching again. From what we know, Yui Ikari's family had good ties to Seele, whereas Gendo Rokubungi was a nobody, and perhaps even poorly reputable if Fuyutsuki's first impression of him is anything to go by. It was even rumored at the time that Gendo had only been getting close to Yui simply to get in close with Seele. Well, whatever he did worked; eventually he got assigned with the Katsuragi Expedition, doing who knows what, but he mysteriously leaves the day before Second Impact. Did he know Second Impact was going to occur? Most definitely. Was he responsible for it? Well, my idea is that it would have inevitably happened anyway with the team just being there; Seele funded the Katsuragi team with that intent, and put Gendo there to just nudge them in the right direction to make sure things went smoothly. Perhaps it was his idea to remove the Lance from Adam? Not too long after, Gendo and Yui became married and he became Gendo Ikari, essentially becoming part of the family, which leads me to my next point: Exactly how close was Yui's family to Seele? This is fanwank territory, but being the crazy cult that it is, maybe Yui was an heiress to some Seele family seat and only family could be part of it, and that's why Gendo became close with her. Like royal families, only those with the blood or those accepted into the family can take power. Sorta adds a twisted new take on the Seele mythology. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 05, 2014 12:01 pm For all we know Yui's dad might be one of the HIC guys who isn't part of the UN council but is part of the ritual circle in EoE, just another of the Seele #whatevers that blink out one by one.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Feb 05, 2014 8:22 pm I think the CI flat-out stated that Yui's dad was a Seele member. As for what happened, I don't think Gendo actually sabotaged the CI but was rather there to make sure it went as planned. Unlike the scientists, he knew that the experiment would lead to disaster and he high-tailed it out of there before all of Antarctica went boom.

[]:             Quote Post by one-eyed » Tue Feb 11, 2014 9:21 am I always found it kind of obvious. He got out of there a day before the fateful experiment with the entire search result which, incidentally, had not finished yet, and, second Fuyutsuki, received a lot of money from SEELE soon after. Nobody gives money to reward luck. Moreover, he refers to the result of the Second Impact as purification from original sin, most genocidal psychopaths likes to refer to their actions as cleansing or purification, is the method of rationalizing their actions. Most not wants to admit that are monsters.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Feb 17, 2014 1:19 pm I certainly wouldn't put it past him.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Feb 17, 2014 1:27 pm While the timing is clearly suspect, we don't actually know that Gendo left of his own accord or if he was perhaps told to return. It's possible that he had no idea what was about to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Feb 17, 2014 1:32 pm My assumption has always been that he anticipated things going badly awry, but was not in a position to exercise anything other than self-preservation. Which may have been simply to get the hell out of Dodge and let a number of his superiors in the organization get taken out, thus providing significant career advancement opportunities in the aftermath.

[]:             Quote Post by one-eyed » Mon Feb 17, 2014 7:25 pm NemZ wrote: While the timing is clearly suspect, we don't actually know that Gendo left of his own accord or if he was perhaps told to return. It's possible that he had no idea what was about to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:03 am When you're trying to get ahead in a secret organization you don't become a whistle blower or pass the buck. If you get caught you shut up and do the time, and the organization will reward your loyalty in kind.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 18, 2014 2:23 am Gendo and Keel talk about how myopic and arrogant scientists are while keeping an eye on the Katsuragi expedition, and Gendo was sent to Antarctica as an auditor, so he clearly wasn't part of the scientific team. On second examination, I find the possibility that Gendo actually sabotaged the experiment remote. He had a vague idea that the whole thing would end in disaster and got out of there, but didn't make it happen. I have the impression that Gendo was more or less in favor of what Seele was doing until after Yui's death and his own elevation to the head of Nerv, at which point the reality of what he was doing struck him like a baseball bat.

[]:             Quote Post by one-eyed » Mon Mar 03, 2014 2:52 pm UrsusArctos wrote: As for what happened, I don't think Gendo actually sabotaged the CI but was rather there to make sure it went as planned. Unlike the scientists, he knew that the experiment would lead to disaster and he high-tailed it out of there before all of Antarctica went boom.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 03, 2014 4:58 pm one-eyed wrote: disdain for scientists.
